title: Ensembles and Cocktails: Robust Finetuning for Natural Language Generation

structured.summary: The paper proposes methods to overcome the trade-off between in-distribution and out-of-distribution performance in fine-tuning pretrained language models for natural language generation. It introduces ensemble and cocktail fine-tuning techniques, leveraging distillation and ensembling, to capture the strengths of both full and lightweight fine-tuning without sacrificing performance in either domain.

strengths.point: Addresses a significant performance trade-off in language model fine-tuning.
strengths.evidence: The introduction clearly outlines the problem of balancing ID and OOD performance using current fine-tuning methods.

strengths.point: Presents a novel adaptation of established methods for NLG tasks.
strengths.evidence: The use of known methods like distillation and ensembling is applied uniquely to achieve robust performance in NLG, as highlighted in the proposed cocktail fine-tuning.

weaknesses.point: Lack of novelty in employed methods.
weaknesses.evidence: Methods like ensembling and student-teacher distillation are described as 'simple, well-known methods,' indicating limited novelty (Introduction).
The optimization process for ensemble weights and cocktail mixture weights is not specified, impacting clarity and reproducibility (Introduction).

weaknesses.point: Unquantified results and lack of comparative metrics.
weaknesses.evidence: The results section does not provide specific data points or benchmarks to substantiate improved performance (Results section).

questions.question: How effective are the proposed methods compared to state-of-the-art models in specific tasks?
questions.motivation: Understanding the comparative performance would highlight the practical utility of the methods and their advancements over existing techniques.

critique_points.point_001: Lack of novelty in employed methods.. Methods like ensembling and student-teacher distillation are described as 'simple, well-known methods,' indicating limited novelty (Introduction)
critique_points.point_002: Insufficient detail on methodological processes.. The optimization process for ensemble weights and cocktail mixture weights is not specified, impacting clarity and reproducibility (Introduction)
critique_points.point_003: Unquantified results and lack of comparative metrics.. The results section does not provide specific data points or benchmarks to substantiate improved performance (Results section)
